**2.**

Denis et al(2020)
suggests that is a technique used my data analyst to determine the correctness
of a program using a set of laid down procedures that must be followed . The
normalisation process is done to produce various positive outcomes. One of
which is it helps analyst in the analysis process to determine the entities
from  a  given set which are not correctly formed. Moreover, it also helps in
selecting entities that could be potentially brought out of a file. According
to NTIC(2026), Normalisation helps prevent repetition of data in order to present
it in an organized form.

However, according
to Geeks for Geeks(2025), it creates a spike in the data complexity mainly
because the knowledge will be difficult to follow through and on the part of the
business they will be an increase in reporting and data analysis which will be cumbersome
and time consuming. Moreover, it isn’t scalable because data addition will have
a series of steps to add.

11 February 2026].

Geeks for

from: [Advantages
and Disadvantages of Normalization - GeeksforGeeks](https://www.geeksforgeeks.org/dbms/advantages-and-disadvantages-of-normalization/) [Accessed 11 February
2026].
